title: Memories Of Cab 804 1

jokes.text: What am I, an information booth?
jokes.setup: Character asks Louie a simple question about John
jokes.punchline: Louie responds with sarcastic rhetorical question instead of just saying no
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie cantankerous response - character-specific crankiness delivered efficiently

jokes.text: He should have been in? I should have been rich. You should have had a better nose. Life is cruel.
jokes.setup: Someone says John should have been back by now
jokes.punchline: Louie escalates from the reasonable to absurd to personal insult to philosophical
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that reveals character - moves from practical to personal attack to mock-philosophy

jokes.text: Hey, get away from that cage, Bobby. We're not insured if he breaks out.
jokes.setup: Someone warns Bobby about getting too close to Louie's cage
jokes.punchline: Treating Louie like a dangerous zoo animal that might escape
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor treating Louie as caged beast - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: He's worse than crabs. That miserable little loaf.
jokes.setup: Louie is angry at John for wrecking the cab
jokes.punchline: Calling someone a 'miserable little loaf' - unexpected food metaphor
jokes.explanation: 'Miserable little loaf' is memorably weird insult that fits Louie's creative crankiness

jokes.text: He's fine till I get my hands on him and my feet.
jokes.setup: Asked if John is hurt in the accident
jokes.punchline: Louie threatens both manual and pedal violence
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie threat with slight twist of specifying both hands and feet

jokes.text: You put her into low, and you know what she'd do? She'd go into low.
jokes.setup: Tony sentimentally describing the special qualities of cab 804
jokes.punchline: The 'special' quality is completely ordinary mechanical function
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimatic punchline - builds expectation for something special, delivers the most mundane thing possible with sincere delivery

jokes.text: It's like she knew you trusted her.
jokes.setup: Tony continues his sentimental tribute to the cab
jokes.punchline: Attributing human emotional intelligence to a taxi that shifts into low gear
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that establishes Tony's sincere but absurd attachment to the mechanical cab

jokes.text: What do you need it for? To protect me from muggers. Oh...
jokes.setup: John asks about the safety shield in flashback
jokes.punchline: Alex's resigned 'Oh...' reaction suggests this was obvious information
jokes.explanation: Simple beat joke - the 'Oh' suggests John should have figured this out himself

jokes.text: I should have known then.
jokes.setup: Alex narrates about meeting John
jokes.punchline: Suggests John's cluelessness about safety shields foreshadowed his accident
jokes.explanation: Connects current accident to past cluelessness with hindsight irony

jokes.text: I was in 804 the night I got held up. That was lucky? I lived.
jokes.setup: Bobby calls getting robbed lucky, someone questions this
jokes.punchline: Darkly notes that survival itself was the lucky part
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect economy - shifts perspective on what constitutes luck

jokes.text: Oh, uh... sorry. I forgot to lock that door. You're going to have to sit in the back. Or the front. Whichever.
jokes.setup: Bobby apologetically tells robbery customer about seating
jokes.punchline: Completely flustered, offers contradictory seating options to his robber
jokes.explanation: Bobby's nervous politeness to his robber creates absurd customer service moment

jokes.text: I just want your fare money, son. No games. Uh, I can't. See, it's locked in a strongbox, and we don't have the keys.
jokes.setup: Robber demands money straightforwardly
jokes.punchline: Bobby gives elaborate excuse instead of just refusing
jokes.explanation: Bobby's overthinking creates unnecessarily complex lie when simple refusal would do

jokes.text: Hey, don't play with me, man. It's in the cigar box under the seat. You've done this before.
jokes.setup: Bobby claims money is locked away securely
jokes.punchline: Robber knows exactly where taxi money is really kept, exposing Bobby's lie
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - the criminal is more knowledgeable about taxi operations than expected

jokes.text: My gun's bigger. You should drop yours.
jokes.setup: Standoff where both have guns drawn
jokes.punchline: Bobby uses playground logic about gun size during armed robbery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant childlike logic applied to life-threatening situation - perfectly captures Bobby's character

jokes.text: See, man, you don't understand. I do this for a living. You don't understand. You're not getting a dime from this cab.
jokes.setup: Robber explains this is his profession
jokes.punchline: Bobby remains completely unmoved by professional criminal credentials
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between professional criminal's reasonable expectations and Bobby's stubborn principles

jokes.text: Now I really believe that we could... We can break through the racial barriers and come to a genuine understanding here, man. You're not getting the money. You pasty-faced, vanilla, lily-white towhead.
jokes.setup: Robber tries diplomatic approach about racial understanding
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons diplomacy for racial insults when Bobby still refuses
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from high-minded racial harmony to creative string of white-person insults - great character hypocrisy

jokes.text: You got a ticket.
jokes.setup: Robber asks why Bobby is laughing during standoff
jokes.punchline: A parking ticket was issued while they were having an armed robbery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd twist - bureaucracy continues during violent crime, perfectly captures NYC life

jokes.text: While we were sitting here we fogged up the windows so much a cop came by, gave us a ticket and didn't even spot us. Now, that's funny.
jokes.setup: Bobby explains the ticket situation
jokes.punchline: The irony that police presence actually helped hide their crime
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up that explains the beautiful irony - law enforcement inadvertently aids crime through bureaucracy

jokes.text: Let's give each other a break and switch guns to the other hand. Okay?
jokes.setup: Both men are tired from holding guns for so long
jokes.punchline: Robber suggests polite solution to continue their armed standoff comfortably
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treating armed robbery like a cooperative endurance contest with mutual courtesy

jokes.text: Hey, man. Hey, you did that on purpose. You got my gun and I got yours. Let's switch.
jokes.setup: They agree to switch gun hands on count of three
jokes.punchline: Bobby somehow tricks the robber into actually switching weapons entirely
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff - Bobby outsmarts the robber through literal interpretation

jokes.text: Uh-uh. Why not? Mine wasn't loaded. Well, m-mine isn't either.
jokes.setup: Bobby refuses to switch back guns
jokes.punchline: Reveals both were bluffing with unloaded weapons the whole time
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes entire standoff - both were essentially playing pretend

jokes.text: They're stuck.
jokes.setup: Trying to get out of the cab
jokes.punchline: Car doors are stuck, trapping them together
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat, extends the absurd situation further

jokes.text: Hey, man, you're nuts. Aw, that's it, man. Man, I... you ain't no fun to stick up, man.
jokes.setup: Robbery has gone completely sideways
jokes.punchline: Robber gives up because Bobby isn't entertaining enough to rob
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - robber treats crime like entertainment and finds Bobby disappointing as a victim

jokes.text: Hey, not so fast. You owe me 38 bucks. For what? The meter was running.
jokes.setup: Robber tries to leave after failed robbery
jokes.punchline: Bobby demands payment for taxi fare during his own robbery
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant reversal - Bobby turns tables completely, treating robber as fare-skipping customer. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: You make it 48. I forgot about the ticket.
jokes.setup: Bobby demands taxi fare from robber
jokes.punchline: Adds the parking ticket fee to the robber's bill
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the absurdity - Bobby's bureaucratic mindset makes robber pay for parking violation

jokes.text: Hey, now I don't have enough for cab fare home. What's your address? 447 West 23rd street. You take me there? No, I just want to tell the cops where you live.
jokes.setup: Robber complains about not having cab fare after paying Bobby
jokes.punchline: Bobby asks for address but only to report him to police, not help him
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation Bobby might help, then reveals civic duty instead

jokes.text: "Heroic Cabby Cracks Down On Crime." Third page, 14 paragraphs, small photo. I'd almost forgotten about it.
jokes.setup: Bobby claims the newspaper coverage was 'nothing to write home about'
jokes.punchline: Then recites specific details showing he remembers it perfectly and was quite proud
jokes.explanation: Nice character reveal - Bobby's false modesty exposed by his detailed recall of his glory moment

jokes.text: You didn't save anybody's life. Sure, I did. You did not. It was no big thing.
jokes.setup: Tony challenges Tony's claim about saving someone
jokes.punchline: Tony simultaneously defends and dismisses his own heroic act
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's conflicted pride - wants credit but also wants to seem humble

jokes.text: Hey, don't do that! Okay. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Passenger announces intention to jump off bridge
jokes.punchline: Tony's casual 'don't do that' is enough to change his mind completely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant minimalist suicide prevention - the most casual possible intervention succeeds completely

jokes.text: That's all there was to it? Yeah. He has the nerve to compare that story with mine. At least I didn't take an hour to tell it.
jokes.setup: Bobby dismisses Tony's brief life-saving story
jokes.punchline: Tony defends it by noting his story's brevity vs Bobby's long robbery tale
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about storytelling length that reveals character competition

jokes.text: Driver, I want you to take him to the Walden School, this address. Now, here's your luggage, and don't lose it. And don't sell it.
jokes.setup: Father giving instructions about his son
jokes.punchline: Has to specifically warn against selling luggage, suggesting past experience
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - the specific warning suggests this kid has sold his belongings before

jokes.text: You a father? I don't think so. Don't be.
jokes.setup: Kid asks Louie about fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Gives anti-parenthood advice based on his own experience being awful
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Louie's self-awareness about being a terrible influence

jokes.text: This is De Palma in 804. Taking a fare to the Walden School... Central Park West. Bully for you, De Palma. Two fares in five hours. A good day.
jokes.setup: Louie reports his fare proudly
jokes.punchline: Dispatcher sarcastically notes this is only his second fare in five hours
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's poor work ethic through dispatcher's sarcastic congratulation

jokes.text: Who's he? My dispatcher. All dispatchers are scum. Remember that.
jokes.setup: Kid asks about the voice on radio
jokes.punchline: Louie calls all dispatchers scum while being one himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Louie disparages his own profession without self-awareness

jokes.text: Hey, I'll give you another 20 if you let me drive. What, do you think I'm crazy? You'd kill somebody. I'd lose my license. 30? Okay.
jokes.setup: Kid offers money to drive, Louie lists obvious dangers
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepts when price goes up by $10
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - all his safety concerns vanish for $10 more, showing his greedy priorities

jokes.text: You sure you know how to do this? You don't steal cars without knowing how to drive them.
jokes.setup: Louie asks if 12-year-old can drive
jokes.punchline: Kid casually mentions car theft as his driving experience
jokes.explanation: Great casual revelation of serious criminal activity from a child - delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: That includes 65 for the transistor radio.
jokes.setup: Kid mentions his expensive outfit, Louie mentions his cost $75
jokes.punchline: Most of Louie's outfit cost was just a radio
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's misplaced priorities - expensive electronics but cheap clothes

jokes.text: You don't mind if I say your mother's a very attractive woman? Very nice bagonzas, if you know what I mean. What are bagonzas? How old are you? 12. Bagonzas are feet.
jokes.setup: Louie makes inappropriate comment about kid's mother using made-up euphemism
jokes.punchline: Quickly lies about what 'bagonzas' means when confronted by 12-year-old
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Louie's inappropriate comment and desperate cover story when caught

jokes.text: All right... go. All 50 states. I can't believe you fell for that old trick.
jokes.setup: Louie gives the signal to start reciting states
jokes.punchline: Kid just says 'All 50 states' literally and claims victory
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay con executed well - kid outsmarts the adult through literal interpretation

jokes.text: Zero-zero.
jokes.setup: Kid bets he can predict exact score of tonight's basketball game before it starts
jokes.punchline: Gives starting score of any game - zero to zero
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection - technically correct answer to impossible-seeming bet

jokes.text: Boy, I wish this was a longer trip.
jokes.setup: Kid has successfully conned Louie twice
jokes.punchline: Regrets not having more time to fleece him further
jokes.explanation: Shows kid's predatory nature - views the cab ride as criminal opportunity

jokes.text: Do you smoke? Camels, unfiltered.
jokes.setup: Kid asks about Louie's smoking habits before breath-holding contest
jokes.punchline: Louie admits to smoking the strongest cigarettes possible
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Louie's terrible health habits before breath contest

jokes.text: Hey, you okay?! You breathed!
jokes.setup: Long breath-holding contest between chain smoker and kid
jokes.punchline: Kid wins through superior lung capacity and youth
jokes.explanation: Predictable outcome but shows physical comedy of Louie's defeat

jokes.text: You take $600 from a child? You call that a great story? He was a monster. And I don't care if it was an old lady's life savings. 600 bucks is a great story.
jokes.setup: Bobby criticizes Louie for taking money from a child
jokes.punchline: Louie defends it and escalates to hypothetical old lady robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - completely unrepentant and escalates to worse hypothetical crime

jokes.text: I mean, we don't know for sure that 804 is totaled. Maybe it was just a fender bender.
jokes.setup: Everyone assumes the worst about the cab accident
jokes.punchline: Alex tries to be optimistic right before John arrives to presumably confirm the worst
jokes.explanation: Setup for dramatic irony - audience knows this optimism is probably misplaced

jokes.text: John enters completely destroyed with no dialogue - visual gag of his devastated state
jokes.setup: Alex just suggested maybe it was just a minor accident
jokes.punchline: John's physical appearance immediately contradicts this hope
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline that immediately deflates Alex's optimism without any dialogue needed

tmdb_overview: As Latka repairs Cab 804, the cabbies reminisce about the odd passengers it carried.
insight: Nostalgia episode leans on character quirks over punchlines, landing 46 jokes at modest density.
